Reduce heat to lowest setting and simmer covered for 20 minutes until water absorbs and rice steams. Taste and add salt as needed.\n\nStep 2: Season & sear the chicken. Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to 3/4\" (2cm) thickness. Sprinkle both sides with salt. Combine equal parts za'atar, garam masala, sumac, and cumin. Liberally coat both sides of chicken. Preheat pan over high heat with oil. Sear chicken to create dark crust without burning, flip to finish cooking. Ensure internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.\n\nStep 3: Assemble the bowl. Layer cooked rice in bowl, drizzle yogurt and hot sauce, add diced chicken layer, then top with pomegranate seeds, almonds, feta, parsley, and red onions as desired. Serves 2 large portions.","alternatives":[{"id":"r056","name":"Basic Risotto with Parmesan","name_zh":"帕尔马干酪意大利烩饭","cuisine":"Italian","cooking_method":"braise","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","rich","savory"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"sauce":["butter","broth","water","salt","black pepper"],"veggie":["onions","garlic","parsley"],"carb":["arborio rice"],"protein":["parmigiano reggiano"]},"ingredients_have":["arborio rice"],"ingredients_need":["butter","onions","garlic","broth","water","parmigiano reggiano","parsley","salt","black pepper"],"ingredients":[{"name":"butter","amount":"28 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"onions","amount":"1/4","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"2 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"arborio rice","amount":"120 g","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"broth","amount":"120 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"water","amount":"240 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"parmigiano reggiano","amount":"40 g","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"parsley","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"black pepper","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Melt butter in pan over medium-low heat. Grate in onion and garlic cloves (or finely mince). Sauté for 1-2 minutes until softened and fragrant. 2. Add rice and stir to coat kernels in fat and lightly toast exterior. Add about 80% of cooking liquid (broth and water), reserving the rest. Turn heat to medium-high and bring to simmer. Stir once or twice, then reduce heat to low. Cover with lid and set timer for 10 minutes. 3. After 10 minutes, stir rice once or twice, cover, and set timer for another 10 minutes. 4. Test rice doneness. Add remaining cooking liquid as needed to reach desired consistency. Add more liquid for loose texture; use less or continue reducing for compact, sticky texture. 5. Stir in Parmigiano and remaining butter until creamy. Season with salt, black pepper, and fresh herbs to taste.","alternatives":[]},{"id":"r057","name":"Fiery Carbonara","name_zh":"香辣培根蛋面","cuisine":"Italian","cooking_method":"braise","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","rich","savory","spicy"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"sauce":["water","cornstarch","olive oil","harissa paste","lemons, juice","aleppo pepper","pasta water, reserved","cayenne pepper"],"protein":["parmigiano reggiano","egg yolks","guanciale"],"carb":["spaghetti, bronze cut"],"veggie":["garlic"]},"ingredients_have":[],"ingredients_need":["water","cornstarch","parmigiano reggiano","olive oil","harissa paste","egg yolks","lemons, juice","aleppo pepper","spaghetti, bronze cut","pasta water, reserved","guanciale","garlic","cayenne pepper"],"ingredients":[{"name":"water","amount":"150 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cornstarch","amount":"10 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"parmigiano reggiano","amount":"100 g","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"olive oil","amount":"15 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"harissa paste","amount":"15 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"egg yolks","amount":"4","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"lemons, juice","amount":"1/2","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"aleppo pepper","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"spaghetti, bronze cut","amount":"a serving","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"pasta water, reserved","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"guanciale","amount":"a handful","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"2 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cayenne pepper","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Make stabilizing gel: add cornstarch and water to saucepan, mix until dissolved, heat to low while whisking to just below simmer. Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Slowly bring up temperature while tossing pasta. Can come to light simmer without breaking. 6. Serve pasta, finish with extra sauce, grated parm and reserved guanciale.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"recipe_card","data":{"event_type":"recipe_card","recipe":{"id":"r108","name":"Sunday Preheat oven to 475°F/246°C. Remove backbone using kitchen shears, cutting along both sides from cavity toward neck. Place chicken breast-side up and press down firmly until it lies flat.\n\nStep 2: Season the chicken. Apply generous salt and pepper to front and back, getting into all crevices. Optional: add herbs like rosemary or thyme. Optionally dry brine for 4 hours to 2 days. Position wire rack on baking sheet, place chicken on top. Add seasoned backbone. Apply thin oil layer to legs and breasts.\n\nStep 3: Roast the chicken. Roast 40-50 minutes. After 20 minutes, check bird; lower temperature to 425°F/218°C if browning too quickly. Use meat thermometer: remove when breast reaches 150°F/65°C and thigh reaches 175°F/80°C. Rest 10 minutes.\n\nStep 4: Make the green sauce. Add jalapeños, cilantro, garlic, lime juice, and salt to blender; blend until finely chopped. Add mayo and yogurt; blend smooth. Slowly drizzle olive oil while blending until emulsified. Taste and adjust seasoning.\n\nStep 5: Carve and serve. Cut leg from breast, separate at joint. Remove wings at joints. Slice breasts on both sides along bone. Serve with green sauce and sides.","alternatives":[{"id":"r115","name":"Chicken with Rosemary Garlic Pan Sauce","name_zh":"迷迭香蒜香煎鸡配锅底酱汁","cuisine":"American","cooking_method":"oven","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","savory","tangy"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["chicken breasts"],"sauce":["salt","chicken stock","cooking oil","cornstarch slurry","black pepper","lemon juice"],"veggie":["rosemary","garlic"]},"ingredients_have":["chicken breasts","chicken stock"],"ingredients_need":["salt","rosemary","garlic","cooking oil","cornstarch slurry","black pepper","lemon juice"],"ingredients":[{"name":"chicken breasts","amount":"1","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"chicken stock","amount":"200 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"rosemary","amount":"5 g","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"1 clove","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cooking oil","amount":"a drizzle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cornstarch slurry","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"black pepper","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"lemon juice","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Thin chicken breast by slicing or pounding flat. Add salt to both sides. Optionally brine for 1+ hours.\n2. Heat pan over medium-high with oil. Sear chicken 1-2 minutes per side until browned. Cook to 155F/68C internal temperature, then rest.\n3. Add garlic and rosemary to bloom 30 seconds. Pour stock and deglaze pan. Reduce liquid by half (2-4 min). Add cornstarch slurry if needed. Season with pepper, lemon juice, and salt.\n4. Slice chicken on bias, spoon sauce over top. Preheat oven to 400°F/204°C. 2. Combine all meatball ingredients in large bowl and mix until evenly combined. 3. Form around 9 large meatballs and place on sheet tray. 4. Roast until they reach 155°F/68°C. 5. Add butter and olive oil to pan over medium-high heat. 6. As butter melts, add garlic cloves followed by spices and let bloom for several seconds before adding tomato paste. 7. Stir sauce base together and add peeled tomatoes and onion. 8. Simmer covered for about 1 hour. 9. Remove onion from sauce and blend until smooth. 10. Taste and adjust seasonings, adding more tomato paste if sauce is too thin. 11. Boil noodles per package instructions until al dente. 12. Bring pan to medium heat and toss serving of noodles with spoonful of sauce and fresh basil. 13. Apply thin oil layer to legs and breasts.\n\nStep 3: Roast the chicken. Roast 40-50 minutes. After 20 minutes, check bird; lower temperature to 425°F/218°C if browning too quickly. Use meat thermometer: remove when breast reaches 150°F/65°C and thigh reaches 175°F/80°C. Rest 10 minutes.\n\nStep 4: Make the green sauce. Add jalapeños, cilantro, garlic, lime juice, and salt to blender; blend until finely chopped. Add mayo and yogurt; blend smooth. Slowly drizzle olive oil while blending until emulsified. Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

vars.input: I have chicken and broccoli. Please help me prepare dinner for two people.

results.error: The primary recommendation (Gochujang Steak Bowl) uses all three user-provided ingredients and needs only a few non-staple extras (gochujang, sesame oil, cucumber, green onions). However, the other two featured recipes are heavily loaded with non-provided ingredients: Za'atar Chicken Bowl requires chicken (not provided) plus many specialty items, and Cumin Beef Pita ignores egg and rice while requiring hummus, pita, feta, pickled beets, and lemon juice. The output as a whole presents recipes that frequently deviate from the user's available ingredients.

Add rice and chicken bouillon, mix, and lightly toast for 2 minutes until grains are coated. Add water, increase heat, and cover to bring to boil. Reduce heat to lowest setting and simmer covered for 20 minutes until water absorbs and rice steams. Taste and add salt as needed.\n\nStep 2: Season & sear the chicken. Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to 3/4\" (2cm) thickness. Sprinkle both sides with salt. Combine equal parts za'atar, garam masala, sumac, and cumin. Liberally coat both sides of chicken. Preheat pan over high heat with oil. Sear chicken to create dark crust without burning, flip to finish cooking. Ensure internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.\n\nStep 3: Assemble the bowl. Layer cooked rice in bowl, drizzle yogurt and hot sauce, add diced chicken layer, then top with pomegranate seeds, almonds, feta, parsley, and red onions as desired. Add rice and stir to coat kernels in fat and lightly toast exterior. Add about 80% of cooking liquid (broth and water), reserving the rest. Turn heat to medium-high and bring to simmer. Stir once or twice, then reduce heat to low. Cover with lid and set timer for 10 minutes. 3. After 10 minutes, stir rice once or twice, cover, and set timer for another 10 minutes. 4. Test rice doneness. Add remaining cooking liquid as needed to reach desired consistency. Add more liquid for loose texture; use less or continue reducing for compact, sticky texture. 5. Stir in Parmigiano and remaining butter until creamy. Make stabilizing gel: add cornstarch and water to saucepan, mix until dissolved, heat to low while whisking to just below simmer. Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Slowly bring up temperature while tossing pasta. Can come to light simmer without breaking. 6. Serve pasta, finish with extra sauce, grated parm and reserved guanciale.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"recipe_card","data":{"event_type":"recipe_card","recipe":{"id":"r006","name":"Gochujang Steak Bowl","name_zh":"辣酱牛排饭碗","cuisine":"Korean","cooking_method":"pan-fry","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["spicy","umami","savory"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["beef sirloin","egg"],"carb":["rice"],"sauce":["gochujang","soy sauce","sesame oil"],"veggie":["cucumber","green onions"]},"ingredients_have":["beef sirloin","rice","egg"],"ingredients_need":["gochujang","soy sauce","sesame oil","cucumber","green onions","sesame seeds"],"ingredients":[{"name":"beef sirloin","amount":"12 oz","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"rice","amount":"1 cup","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"gochujang","amount":"2 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"soy sauce","amount":"1 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"sesame oil","amount":"1 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cucumber","amount":"1","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"green onions","amount":"3 stalks","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"sesame seeds","amount":"1 tbsp","pcsv":[]},{"name":"egg","amount":"2","pcsv":["protein"]}],"instructions":"Cook rice. Thinly slice the steak against the grain into small strips. Add salt and ground cumin over top. Mix to evenly coat the beef. Preheat a saute pan over high heat. Add about a spoonful of oil to coat the pan, then add beef to the pan. Allow the beef to sear undisturbed on one side to develop a crust and deep browning.\n\nStep 2: Finish the beef & steam the pita. Once the beef has lots of color on one side, flip the beef and continue searing until mostly cooked through. Add the pita bread on top of the meat, cover, and steam the bread for a couple of minutes to soften. Gather or prep any other ingredients you'll be using in the pita. Once the pita is warmed through, remove & turn off the heat. Taste the beef and adjust the seasoning if needed.\n\nStep 3: Assemble and serve. Slice open the pita. Add hummus to both sides of the pita bread. Layer in the pickled items, feta cheese, and a portion of beef (about 1/3 of the beef). Add a spritz of lemon and a sprinkle of diced raw onion. Repeat with remaining sandwiches. Before serving, wrap the sandwich in foil to steam and soften for about a minute. Add rice and chicken bouillon, mix, and lightly toast for 2 minutes until grains are coated. Add water, increase heat, and cover to bring to boil. Reduce heat to lowest setting and simmer covered for 20 minutes until water absorbs and rice steams. Taste and add salt as needed.\n\nStep 2: Season & sear the chicken. Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to 3/4\" (2cm) thickness. Sprinkle both sides with salt. Combine equal parts za'atar, garam masala, sumac, and cumin. Liberally coat both sides of chicken. Preheat pan over high heat with oil. Sear chicken to create dark crust without burning, flip to finish cooking. Ensure internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.\n\nStep 3: Assemble the bowl. Stir once or twice, then reduce heat to low. Cover with lid and set timer for 10 minutes. 3. After 10 minutes, stir rice once or twice, cover, and set timer for another 10 minutes. 4. Test rice doneness. Add remaining cooking liquid as needed to reach desired consistency. Add more liquid for loose texture; use less or continue reducing for compact, sticky texture. 5. Stir in Parmigiano and remaining butter until creamy. Make stabilizing gel: add cornstarch and water to saucepan, mix until dissolved, heat to low while whisking to just below simmer. Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Slowly bring up temperature while tossing pasta. Can come to light simmer without breaking. 6. Thinly slice the steak against the grain into small strips. Add salt and ground cumin over top. Mix to evenly coat the beef. Preheat a saute pan over high heat. Add about a spoonful of oil to coat the pan, then add beef to the pan. Allow the beef to sear undisturbed on one side to develop a crust and deep browning.\n\nStep 2: Finish the beef & steam the pita. Once the beef has lots of color on one side, flip the beef and continue searing until mostly cooked through. Add the pita bread on top of the meat, cover, and steam the bread for a couple of minutes to soften. Gather or prep any other ingredients you'll be using in the pita. Once the pita is warmed through, remove & turn off the heat. Taste the beef and adjust the seasoning if needed.\n\nStep 3: Assemble and serve. Slice open the pita. Add hummus to both sides of the pita bread. Layer in the pickled items, feta cheese, and a portion of beef (about 1/3 of the beef). Add a spritz of lemon and a sprinkle of diced raw onion. Repeat with remaining sandwiches. Before serving, wrap the sandwich in foil to steam and soften for about a minute. (3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?

Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).

1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

Prep the noodles and sauce: Soak noodles to rehydrate per package directions. Mix all sauce ingredients in a container until well combined. 2. Stir fry the eggs and vegetables: Heat wok or large pan over high heat with oil. Add beaten egg, swirl around pan until fluffy with browning, chop and set aside. Add more oil, garlic, and white pepper; bloom for 30-45 seconds. Add chopped broccolini, stir fry 1-2 minutes, add a drizzle of sauce, then remove to bowl with eggs. 3. Brown the chicken: Add oil to wok, add chicken slices, brown 2 minutes per side, add sauce for color and seasoning. 4. Bring it all together: Add broccolini and egg back to wok with drained noodles. Add enough sauce to coat, stir fry 5-7 minutes until noodles soften and absorb sauce. Soak the noodles in hot water according to package directions. Stir tamarind paste, fish sauce, palm sugar, and soy sauce until sugar dissolves.\n2. Set wok over high heat with oil. Sear protein until browned and cooked through. Remove and set aside. Add oil, crack in eggs, scramble until cooked, set aside with protein. Wipe wok.\n3. Drain noodles. Add oil to wok, add dried shrimp and bloom 30 seconds until fragrant. Add garlic and ginger, stir fry 15-30 seconds. Add noodles, reserved egg and protein, and sauce. Toss until coated.\n4. Add scallions and bamboo shoots in final minute. Taste and adjust with fish sauce, sugar, or lime. Serve with peanuts, lime wedges, and green onions.","alternatives":[]},{"id":"r074","name":"Thai Chile Pork & Cabbage Stir Fry","name_zh":"泰式辣椒猪肉炒卷心菜","cuisine":"Thai","cooking_method":"stir-fry","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["aromatic","spicy","umami"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["ground pork","eggs"],"veggie":["cabbage","thai chilies","shallots"],"sauce":["salt","cooking oil","fish sauce","msg","sesame oil","black sesame seeds"]},"ingredients_have":[],"ingredients_need":["ground pork","cabbage","salt","thai chilies","shallots","cooking oil","fish sauce","msg","sesame oil","eggs","black sesame seeds"],"ingredients":[{"name":"ground pork","amount":"225 g","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"cabbage","amount":"1/4, thinly sliced","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"thai chilies","amount":"3-8, thinly sliced","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"shallots","amount":"1, thinly sliced","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"cooking oil","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"fish sauce","amount":"a spoonful","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"msg","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"sesame oil","amount":"a drizzle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"eggs","amount":"1, fried","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"black sesame seeds","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"1. Brown the pork: Preheat a pan over medium-high heat. Add the ground pork and press it into the hot pan, making a thin even layer across the hot surface. Allow the pork to sear undisturbed until the bottom begins to brown and take on some color. Using a potato masher, fork, or spatula, break up the pork into small pebble-sized bits. 2. Add the cabbage and steam: Once the pork is mostly cooked through, add in the sliced cabbage. Stir the cabbage into the meat to ensure nothing is burning on the bottom of the pan. Cover to steam and soften the cabbage for a minute or two. Remove the cover. Sauté for another minute or two to get more browning on the meat and edges of the cabbage and evaporate any excess moisture. 3. Finish with aromatics and sauces: Push the cabbage and pork mixture to one side of the pan. Add a drizzle of oil to the open space and add in the shallot and Thai chilies to bloom for about 30 seconds before mixing everything together. Add the fish sauce to deglaze any browning from the bottom of the pan. Turn off the heat. Add a drizzle of sesame oil and a sprinkle of MSG. Taste and adjust as needed. 4. Plate and serve: Serve with a fried egg and a sprinkle of sesame seeds for garnish.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"explanation","data":{"event_type":"explanation","text":"You’ve got a solid Add rice and chicken bouillon, mix, and lightly toast for 2 minutes until grains are coated. Add water, increase heat, and cover to bring to boil. Reduce heat to lowest setting and simmer covered for 20 minutes until water absorbs and rice steams. Taste and add salt as needed.\n\nStep 2: Season & sear the chicken. Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to 3/4\" (2cm) thickness. Sprinkle both sides with salt. Combine equal parts za'atar, garam masala, sumac, and cumin. Liberally coat both sides of chicken. Preheat pan over high heat with oil. Sear chicken to create dark crust without burning, flip to finish cooking. Ensure internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.\n\nStep 3: Assemble the bowl. Stir once or twice, then reduce heat to low. Cover with lid and set timer for 10 minutes. 3. After 10 minutes, stir rice once or twice, cover, and set timer for another 10 minutes. 4. Test rice doneness. Add remaining cooking liquid as needed to reach desired consistency. Add more liquid for loose texture; use less or continue reducing for compact, sticky texture. 5. Stir in Parmigiano and remaining butter until creamy. Make stabilizing gel: add cornstarch and water to saucepan, mix until dissolved, heat to low while whisking to just below simmer. Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.\n\nStep 3: Assemble the bowl. Make stabilizing gel: add cornstarch and water to saucepan, mix until dissolved, heat to low while whisking to just below simmer. Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. tests.description: C3: vague input — no ingredients, no party size

vars.input: I want to cook something

assert.value:
The user gave a vague prompt. Score 1-5 on three criteria, return the minimum:
(1) Acknowledgment: does the response recognize the input is underspecified?

(3) Usefulness: if assumptions were made, is the output still usable? If clarifications were asked, are they the right ones (ingredients on hand, party size, dietary constraints)?
Return the overall score.

Return the score.
